title: Maxime-Arnaud Keable
content:
## Maxime-Arnaud Keable
People.ContactEmail mkeable@mccarthy.ca
- Dalhousie University
Maxime delivers high-level strategic guidance to clients, leveraging deep insights across the government, education, regulatory, and natural resources sectors.
Maxime-Arnaud Keable is a partner in our Litigation & Dispute Resolution Group and is based in our Québec City office. A seasoned litigator well-respected throughout the Québec legal community, he brings more than 17 years of experience representing clients in complex commercial disputes, with a particular focus on public and administrative law.
Maxime delivers high-level strategic guidance to clients navigating public law, drawing on deep industry insight across the government, education, regulation, and natural resources sectors.
He is committed to developing a deep understanding of his clients’ businesses and the challenges they face, enabling him to craft creative and effective strategies that lead to practical solutions—whether in the Québec region or across the country.
Maxime regularly advises public institutions, professional regulators, and private companies on matters involving government contracts, regulatory compliance, and judicial review. His practice spans challenges to government decrees, municipal by-laws, and disciplinary proceedings, as well as commercial litigation before civil courts and arbitration panels.
His recent work includes:
- Representing the President of the Quebec National Assembly in an intervention before the Quebec Court of Appeal in the Bill 21 matter;
- Representing a private company before the Quebec Court of Appeal in a request by the Autorité des marchés publics to quash the Superior Court judgment granting a stay of a decision forbidding all public contracts;
- Representing a hydroelectric dam valve manufacturer in a commercial arbitration concerning liability for valve deficiencies in a major infrastructure project in British Columbia.
- Representing Tessier Ltée in a constitutional case before the Supreme Court of Canada addressing federal jurisdiction over labour relations in stevedoring operations.
- Representing the Ordre des comptables professionnels agréés du Québec in a Supreme Court intervention concerning the interpretation of Québec’s professional legislation.
- Representing the Government of Québec in a conventional arbitration on compensation for over 1,100 unionized government lawyers and notaries, involving expert testimony and public sector negotiation strategy.
- Representing the Government of Québec before a working parity committee tasked with evaluating the distinct status of government lawyers and notaries, including comparative analysis and extensive evidentiary hearings.
- Representing 50 ambulance companies in an appeal for judicial review and declaratory relief against the Minister of Health, challenging the validity of government decrees and a service contract on administrative law grounds.
- Representing a Canadian bank in a $5.9-million liability claim involving alleged conflicts of interest and the scope of a financial institution’s duty of loyalty and care.
- Representing real estate developers before the Québec Court of Appeal in a dispute concerning compliance with municipal by-laws and neighbourhood disturbance regulations.
- Representing real estate developers charged under a municipal by-law for tenant-related noise disturbances, resulting in their acquittal due to the by-law’s improper scope.
- Representing the Tribunal administratif du logement in a judicial review concerning the non-renewal of an administrative judge’s mandate and related psychological harassment claims.
- Representing a Montréal university in a proposed class action filed by students from 15 Québec universities seeking partial tuition refunds due to COVID-19 disruptions.
- Representing Construction Norascon inc. in a judicial review before the Superior Court of Québec challenging a ministerial decision under the Mining Act regarding exclusive surface mineral leases.
He also lends his expertise to the community, serving on the board of Maisons des Lucioles, a non-profit organization that provides parents of young people with special needs in the Québec City region a range of services, including babysitting, adapted leisure activities, and short-term accommodation services.
Maxime received his LLB from Université Laval in 2007 and his common law degree from Dalhousie in 2008. He was called to the Quebec bar in 2008 and is president of the Comité sur la Formation for the Barreau de Québec.
